MINI MINUTES FOR APR-18-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ALAN BRAVERMAN AND ANGELA BRAVERMAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACT SUPPORTING THEIR ASSERTED BELIEF THAT "THAT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO GRANTOR GRANTEE INDEX LISTS THE BRAVERMANS AS GRANTORS OF THE DEED OF TRUST ENCUMBERING 42 HOTALING PLACE." THIS FACTUAL ALLEGATION MAY BE INCLUDED IN THE BODY OF THE COMPLAINT OR PLAINTIFF MAY ATTACH A COPY OF THE GRANTOR GRANTEE INDEX SO SHOWING. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AND REPLY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. THE COURT DOES NOT REACH THE ISSUE OF STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AT THIS TIME, HOWEVER SHOULD PLAINTIFF WISH TO MAKE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS TO SUPPORT DELAYED DISCOVERY, PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE LEAVE TO DO SO. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-05-2024 Continued To Jun-12-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Nathan U. Desai; Donahue Fitzgerald LLP; 925-746-7770; appearing as counsel for plaintiff JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.
Harlye S. Carlton; Newmeyer & Dillion LLP; 925-988-3200; appearing as counsel for defendants Alan M. Braverman and Angela Heather Braverman.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 42 HOTALING PLACE LLC'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fact supporting their asserted belief that "that the City and County of San Francisco grantor grantee index lists the Bravermans as grantors of the Deed of Trust encumbering 42 Hotaling Place." This factual allegation may be included in the body of the complaint or Plaintiff may attach a copy of the grantor grantee index so showing. The Court does not reach the issue of statute of limitations at this time, however should plaintiff wish to make additional allegations to support delayed discovery, Plaintiff shall have leave to do so.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Nathan U. Desai; Donahue Fitzgerald LLP; 925-746-7770; appearing as counsel for plaintiff JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.
Harlye S. Carlton; Newmeyer & Dillion LLP; 925-988-3200; appearing as counsel for defendants Alan M. Braverman and Angela Heather Braverman.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALAN BRAVERMAN AND ANGELA BRAVERMAN'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege fact supporting their asserted belief that "that the City and County of San Francisco grantor grantee index lists the Bravermans as grantors of the Deed of Trust encumbering 42 Hotaling Place." This factual allegation may be included in the body of the complaint or Plaintiff may attach a copy of the grantor grantee index so showing. Plaintiff's objection to Defendant's request for judicial notice and reply declaration is sustained. The Court does not reach the issue of statute of limitations at this time, however should plaintiff wish to make additional allegations to support delayed discovery, Plaintiff shall have leave to do so.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
